description: This plot demonstrates the tensile strength (TS, in MPa) of 8.5Mn-0.2C (wt.%) austenite-ferrite low-density steels as a function of annealing temperature (T, in °C, ranging from 800 °C to 1000 °C), with variables including alloy composition (6 wt.% Al, 6 wt.% Al with extra Si, 7 wt.% Al) and annealing duration (1 min, 136 sec, 5 min). Green curves correspond to 6Al alloys, red curves to 6Al+XSi alloys, and blue curves to 7Al alloys, with different markers for different annealing times. For all alloy groups, TS consistently decreases with increasing annealing temperature. At the same annealing temperature and duration, 6Al+XSi alloys show the highest TS, followed by 7Al alloys, while 6Al alloys have the lowest TS. For each composition, longer annealing time leads to lower TS. This figure supports that annealing parameters (temperature, holding time) and alloying (Al content, Si addition) can be tailored to adjust the tensile strength of such low-density steels for automotive lightweighting applications.
